packages.name: pandas
packages.synopsis: Package pandas provides pandas-style data structures for Go: a typed, one-dimensional Series and a two-dimensional DataFrame, built entirely on the Go standard library.
packages.doc:
Package pandas provides pandas-style data structures for Go: a typed,
one-dimensional Series and a two-dimensional DataFrame, built entirely on the
Go standard library.

# Series

A Series is a named, typed column of values with an associated index and
first-class support for missing values (NA). The supported element types
(see DType) are Float64, Int64, String and Bool, plus a fallback Object type
for anything else. Values are coerced to the column dtype on construction; a
nil input or a value that cannot be coerced becomes NA.

	s := pandas.NewSeries("price", []any{10.0, 20.0, nil, 40.0})
	mean, ok := s.Mean() // 23.333..., true (NA is skipped)

Series offers element access (At, Values, IsNA), transformation (Apply, Map,
FillNA, DropNA, Sort, Filter, Head, Tail), set operations (Unique,
ValueCounts) and reductions (Sum, Mean, Min, Max, Std, Count).

# DataFrame

A DataFrame is an ordered set of equal-length named columns sharing one row
index. Construct one from Series (NewDataFrame), a map of column to values
(FromMap), a slice of structs (FromRecords) or CSV data (ReadCSV,
ReadCSVFile). Write it back out with WriteCSV and WriteCSVFile.

	df, _ := pandas.FromMap(map[string][]any{
	    "city": {"NYC", "LA", "NYC"},
	    "temp": {31.0, 28.0, 33.0},
	}, []string{"city", "temp"})

# Selection and indexing

Columns are selected by name (Select, Col, Drop). Rows are selected by
position range (ILoc, Head, Tail), by index label (Loc), by explicit
positions (Take) or by a boolean mask (Filter, FilterFunc). The Row type
gives a keyed, read-only view of a single row.

# Transformation

DataFrames support adding and replacing columns (WithColumn), dropping and
renaming (Drop, Rename), sorting by one or more keys (SortBy), and missing
value handling (FillNA, DropNA).

# GroupBy and aggregation

GroupBy partitions a DataFrame by one or more key columns, with deterministic
group ordering. Aggregate with the AggFunc set — Sum, Mean, Min, Max, Count,
Std — either through the typed convenience methods or the general Agg method,
which computes several aggregations at once.

	gb, _ := df.GroupBy("city")
	means, _ := gb.Mean("temp") // one row per city

# Join and merge

Merge joins two DataFrames on a shared key column with inner or left join
semantics (InnerJoin, LeftJoin). Colliding non-key columns are disambiguated
with _left and _right suffixes.

# Describe

Describe returns count, mean, std, min and max for every numeric column.

# Determinism

Every operation produces a stable, reproducible ordering: grouping,
value_counts and sorting all break ties deterministically, and missing
values sort last.

types.name: DType
types.doc: DType enumerates the element types a Series can hold.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the human readable name of the dtype.

types.name: DataFrame
types.doc:
DataFrame is an ordered collection of equal-length named columns (each a
Series) sharing a single row index.

funcs.name: Concat
funcs.doc:
Concat vertically stacks DataFrames into a single frame. The output columns
are the union of the inputs' columns, ordered by first appearance; a frame
lacking a column contributes missing values for it. The output index is the
concatenation of the inputs' index labels. Concat of no frames returns an
empty DataFrame.

funcs.name: FromMap
funcs.doc:
FromMap builds a DataFrame from a map of column name to values. Because Go
map iteration is unordered, the resulting columns are ordered by the supplied
order slice; any names present in data but absent from order are appended in
sorted order for determinism.

funcs.name: FromRecords
funcs.doc:
FromRecords builds a DataFrame from a slice of structs. Exported fields become
columns, in declaration order, using the field name (or the `pandas` struct
tag when present). A tag of "-" skips the field.

funcs.name: NewDataFrame
funcs.doc:
NewDataFrame builds a DataFrame from an ordered list of columns. Every column
must have the same length; the first column's length defines the number of
rows. Column names must be unique. The shared index is taken from the first
column, or the default positional index when there are no columns.

funcs.name: ReadCSV
funcs.doc:
ReadCSV parses CSV data from r into a DataFrame. Column dtypes are inferred:
a column is Int64 if every present value parses as an integer, Float64 if
every present value parses as a float, Bool if every value is a boolean
literal, and String otherwise.

funcs.name: ReadCSVFile
funcs.doc: ReadCSVFile reads and parses a CSV file at path.

methods.name: Abs
methods.doc:
Abs returns a copy of the DataFrame with every numeric column replaced by its
element-wise absolute value. Non-numeric columns are copied unchanged.

methods.name: Col
methods.doc: Col returns the named column and whether it exists.

methods.name: Copy
methods.doc: Copy returns a deep copy of the DataFrame.

methods.name: Corr
methods.doc:
Corr returns the Pearson correlation matrix of the numeric columns. The result
is a square DataFrame with one row per numeric column (row index labelled by
column name) and one column per numeric column. Pairs with fewer than two
jointly present observations, or a column with zero variance, yield a missing
cell; the diagonal is 1 for columns with non-zero variance.

methods.name: Describe
methods.doc:
Describe returns summary statistics (count, mean, std, min, max) for every
numeric column. The result has one row per statistic, labelled by the "stat"
column, and one column per numeric source column.

methods.name: Drop
methods.doc:
Drop returns a new DataFrame without the named columns. Missing names are
ignored.

methods.name: DropDuplicates
methods.doc:
DropDuplicates returns a new DataFrame with duplicate rows removed, keeping the
first occurrence of each distinct row and preserving order. Two rows are equal
when all their columns are equal, with missing values matching missing values.

methods.name: DropNA
methods.doc:
DropNA returns a new DataFrame with rows containing any missing value
removed.

methods.name: FillNA
methods.doc:
FillNA returns a new DataFrame with missing values in the named column filled.
When column is empty every column is filled with value.

methods.name: Filter
methods.doc:
Filter returns the rows where mask is true. The mask length must equal the
number of rows.

methods.name: FilterFunc
methods.doc: FilterFunc returns the rows for which pred, applied to a Row view, is true.

methods.name: GroupBy
methods.doc:
GroupBy groups the DataFrame by the given key columns. Groups are ordered
deterministically by their key values.

methods.name: HasColumn
methods.doc: HasColumn reports whether a column with the given name exists.

methods.name: Head
methods.doc: Head returns the first n rows.

methods.name: ILoc
methods.doc:
ILoc returns the half-open range of rows [start, end) by position, mirroring
pandas iloc slicing. Out-of-range bounds are clamped.

methods.name: Index
methods.doc: Index returns a copy of the row labels.

methods.name: Loc
methods.doc:
Loc returns the rows whose index label is in labels, preserving the order of
labels. Labels not present in the index are skipped.

methods.name: Max
methods.doc:
Max returns the column-wise maximum of every numeric column as a Series
indexed by column name.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc:
Mean returns the column-wise mean of every numeric column as a Series indexed
by column name.

methods.name: Median
methods.doc:
Median returns the column-wise median of every numeric column as a Series
indexed by column name.

methods.name: Merge
methods.signature: func (df *DataFrame) Merge(right *DataFrame, on string, how JoinType) (*DataFrame, error)
methods.doc:
Merge joins df (left) with right on the given key column, which must exist in
both frames. Non-key columns that collide are suffixed with "_left" and
"_right". The output row order is deterministic: left rows in order, and for
each left row its matching right rows in their original order.

methods.name: Min
methods.doc:
Min returns the column-wise minimum of every numeric column as a Series
indexed by column name.

methods.name: MustCol
methods.doc: MustCol returns the named column and panics if it is absent.

methods.name: Names
methods.doc: Names returns the column names in order.

methods.name: NumCols
methods.doc: NumCols returns the number of columns.

methods.name: NumRows
methods.doc: NumRows returns the number of rows.

methods.name: Nunique
methods.doc:
Nunique returns the number of distinct present values in every column as an
Int64 Series indexed by column name. Unlike the numeric reductions it covers
columns of all dtypes.

methods.name: Rename
methods.doc:
Rename returns a new DataFrame with columns renamed according to mapping
(old name -> new name). Names absent from the mapping are unchanged.

methods.name: ResetIndex
methods.doc:
ResetIndex returns a new DataFrame whose current index labels are inserted as
a leading column named "index" and whose row index is replaced by the default
positional index 0..n-1.

methods.name: Round
methods.doc:
Round returns a copy of the DataFrame with every numeric column rounded to the
given number of decimal places. Non-numeric columns are copied unchanged.

methods.name: Row
methods.doc: Row returns a view of the row at position i.

methods.name: Select
methods.doc:
Select returns a new DataFrame containing only the named columns, in the
order given. An error is returned if any name is missing.

methods.name: SetIndex
methods.doc:
SetIndex returns a new DataFrame using the values of the named column as the
row index; that column is removed from the result. An error is returned if
the column does not exist.

methods.name: Shape
methods.doc: Shape returns the number of rows and columns.

methods.name: SortBy
methods.doc:
SortBy returns a new DataFrame sorted by the named columns. The ascending
slice, when non-nil, gives the direction per key; missing entries default to
ascending. Sorting is stable and missing values sort last.

methods.name: Std
methods.doc:
Std returns the column-wise sample standard deviation of every numeric column
as a Series indexed by column name.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders the DataFrame as an aligned text table of its named columns.
The row index is not shown; use Index to inspect the labels. Cells are left
aligned and the final column carries no trailing padding, so the output is
stable for use in tests and examples.

methods.name: Sum
methods.doc:
Sum returns the column-wise sum of every numeric column as a Series indexed by
column name, mirroring the default (axis=0) pandas DataFrame.sum.

methods.name: Tail
methods.doc: Tail returns the last n rows.

methods.name: Take
methods.doc: Take returns the rows at the given positions, in order.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc:
Transpose returns a new DataFrame with rows and columns swapped: each original
column name becomes a row index label and each original row (identified by its
index label, formatted as text) becomes a column. All resulting columns have
Object dtype because a row may mix source dtypes. An error is returned if the
original index labels are not unique, since they must form distinct column
names.

methods.name: Var
methods.doc:
Var returns the column-wise sample variance of every numeric column as a
Series indexed by column name.

methods.name: WithColumn
methods.doc:
WithColumn returns a new DataFrame with col added (or replaced if a column of
the same name already exists). The column length must match the row count of
a non-empty frame.

methods.name: WriteCSV
methods.doc:
WriteCSV writes the DataFrame to w as CSV, including a header row. Missing
values are written as empty fields.

methods.name: WriteCSVFile
methods.doc: WriteCSVFile writes the DataFrame to a CSV file at path.

types.name: GroupBy
types.doc: GroupBy is a lazy grouping of a DataFrame by one or more key columns.

methods.name: Agg
methods.doc:
Agg applies aggregations to columns. The spec maps a source column name to
the aggregations to compute for it. The result DataFrame has the key columns
followed by one column per (source column, aggregation) named
"<column>_<agg>", one row per group in deterministic order.

methods.name: Count
methods.doc:
Count returns the per-group count of the given columns (or of every non-key
column when none are named).

methods.name: Groups
methods.doc: Groups returns the number of groups.

methods.name: Max
methods.doc: Max aggregates the given columns with AggMax.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean aggregates the given columns with AggMean.

methods.name: Min
methods.doc: Min aggregates the given columns with AggMin.

methods.name: Std
methods.doc: Std aggregates the given columns with AggStd.

methods.name: Sum
methods.doc:
Sum aggregates the given columns with AggSum. It is a convenience wrapper
around Agg.

types.name: ReadCSVOptions
Row
types.doc: Row is a read-only view of a single row, keyed by column name.

methods.name: Float
methods.doc: Float returns the named column value as a float64.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc:
Get returns the value of the named column in this row and whether it is
present (not NA).

methods.name: Label
methods.doc: Label returns the index label of this row.

types.name: Series
types.doc:
Series is a named, typed one-dimensional column of values with an associated
index. Missing values (NA) are tracked with a validity mask, so a Series of
any dtype can represent absent data without a sentinel value.

funcs.name: NewSeries
funcs.doc:
NewSeries builds a Series named name from values. The dtype is inferred from
the first non-nil element (defaulting to Object for an all-nil input) and
every element is coerced to that dtype. A nil element, or one that cannot be
coerced, becomes a missing value.

funcs.name: NewSeriesTyped
funcs.signature: func NewSeriesTyped(name string, dt DType, values []any) *Series
funcs.doc: NewSeriesTyped builds a Series with an explicit dtype, coercing each value.

methods.name: Abs
methods.doc:
Abs returns a copy of the series with every present numeric value replaced by
its absolute value. Missing values are preserved. The result dtype matches
the source for Int64 columns and is Float64 otherwise.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc:
Add returns the element-wise sum of two series, aligned by position up to the
shorter length. Positions where either operand is missing become missing. The
result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: Apply
methods.doc:
Apply returns a new Series with fn applied to every present value. Missing
values are passed through untouched. The result dtype is re-inferred.

methods.name: ArgMax
methods.doc:
ArgMax returns the position of the maximum present numeric value and whether
one existed. The first occurrence wins on ties.

methods.name: ArgMin
methods.doc:
ArgMin returns the position of the minimum present numeric value and whether
one existed. The first occurrence wins on ties.

methods.name: Astype
methods.doc:
Astype returns a copy of the series converted to dtype dt. Values that cannot
be coerced to the target dtype become missing, matching the coercion rules
used at construction time.

methods.name: At
methods.doc: At returns the value at position i and whether it is present (not NA).

methods.name: Between
methods.doc:
Between returns a boolean mask reporting, per position, whether the present
numeric value lies in the closed interval [low, high]. Missing or
non-numeric values yield false.

methods.name: Clip
methods.doc:
Clip returns a copy with present numeric values constrained to the closed
interval [lower, upper]. Missing values are preserved and the result dtype is
Float64.

methods.name: Copy
methods.doc: Copy returns a deep copy of the series.

methods.name: Corr
methods.doc:
Corr returns the Pearson correlation coefficient between this series and
other, computed over positions where both hold a present numeric value. It
reports false when fewer than two such positions exist or either series has
zero variance.

methods.name: Count
methods.doc: Count returns the number of present (non-NA) values.

methods.name: Cov
methods.doc:
Cov returns the sample covariance (n-1 denominator) between this series and
other, computed over positions where both hold a present numeric value. It
reports false when fewer than two such positions exist.

methods.name: CumMax
methods.doc:
CumMax returns the cumulative maximum of the present numeric values. Missing
values remain missing and are skipped. The result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: CumMin
methods.doc:
CumMin returns the cumulative minimum of the present numeric values. Missing
values remain missing and are skipped. The result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: CumProd
methods.doc:
CumProd returns the cumulative product of the present numeric values. Missing
values remain missing and are skipped. The result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: CumSum
methods.doc:
CumSum returns the cumulative sum of the present numeric values. Missing
values remain missing and are skipped when accumulating, mirroring the
pandas Series.cumsum semantics. The result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: DType
methods.doc: DType returns the element type of the series.

methods.name: Diff
methods.doc:
Diff returns the first discrete difference: element i minus element i-n. The
first n positions, and any position where either operand is missing, are set
to missing. The result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: Div
methods.doc:
Div returns the element-wise quotient (s / other), aligned by position up to
the shorter length. A zero divisor yields a missing value. The result dtype
is Float64.

methods.name: DropNA
methods.doc: DropNA returns a copy with missing values removed, preserving index labels.

methods.name: FillNA
methods.doc:
FillNA returns a copy with every missing value replaced by value (coerced to
the series dtype).

methods.name: Filter
methods.doc: Filter returns the rows where mask is true. The mask must match the length.

methods.name: Head
methods.doc: Head returns the first n rows (all rows when n exceeds the length).

methods.name: Index
methods.doc: Index returns a copy of the row labels.

methods.name: IsIn
methods.doc:
IsIn returns a boolean mask reporting, per position, whether the present
value equals one of the given values. Comparison uses the canonical stored
representation, so numeric values are matched after coercion to the column
dtype.

methods.name: IsNA
methods.doc: IsNA reports, per position, whether the value is missing.

methods.name: Len
methods.doc: Len returns the number of elements.

methods.name: Map
methods.doc: Map is an alias for Apply, mirroring the pandas Series.map method.

methods.name: Max
methods.doc: Max returns the maximum of the present numeric values.

methods.name: Mean
methods.doc: Mean returns the arithmetic mean of the present numeric values.

methods.name: Median
methods.doc:
Median returns the median (0.5 quantile) of the present numeric values and
whether any existed.

methods.name: Min
methods.doc: Min returns the minimum of the present numeric values.

methods.name: Mode
methods.doc:
Mode returns the most frequently occurring present value(s), in ascending
order. When several values tie for the highest frequency all of them are
returned. An empty slice is returned when there are no present values.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the element-wise product of two series, aligned by position up to
the shorter length. The result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: NLargest
methods.doc:
NLargest returns the n largest present values as a new series, ordered from
largest to smallest, preserving each value's original index label. Ties are
broken by original position. When n exceeds the number of present values all
of them are returned.

methods.name: NSmallest
methods.doc:
NSmallest returns the n smallest present values as a new series, ordered from
smallest to largest, preserving each value's original index label. Ties are
broken by original position. When n exceeds the number of present values all
of them are returned.

methods.name: Name
methods.doc: Name returns the series name.

methods.name: PctChange
methods.doc:
PctChange returns the fractional change between the current and prior
element: (x[i] - x[i-1]) / x[i-1]. The first position, positions with a
missing operand, and divisions by zero are set to missing. The result dtype
is Float64.

methods.name: Prod
methods.doc:
Prod returns the product of the present numeric values and whether any
existed.

methods.name: Quantile
methods.doc:
Quantile returns the q-th quantile (0 <= q <= 1) of the present numeric
values using linear interpolation between the two nearest ranks, matching the
default pandas Series.quantile behaviour. It reports false when there are no
present values or q is out of range.

methods.name: Rank
methods.doc:
Rank returns the ranks of the present numeric values using the average
method, matching the pandas Series.rank default: values are ranked from 1
(smallest) and tied values share the mean of the ranks they span. Missing
values remain missing. The result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: RankBy
methods.doc:
RankBy returns the ranks of the present numeric values, resolving ties
according to method, one of:

  - "average": tied values share the mean of the ranks they span (the default).
  - "min":     tied values all take the lowest rank in the group.
  - "max":     tied values all take the highest rank in the group.
  - "first":   ties are broken by original position, giving distinct ranks.
  - "dense":   like "min" but group ranks always increase by one, leaving no
    gaps after ties.

These mirror the pandas Series.rank method= argument. Missing values remain
missing and the result dtype is Float64. An unrecognised method falls back to
"average".

methods.name: Rename
methods.doc: Rename returns a copy of the series with a new name.

methods.name: Round
methods.doc:
Round returns a copy with every present numeric value rounded to the given
number of decimal places. Ties (a value exactly halfway between two
candidates) are resolved to the nearest even digit — the round-half-to-even
("banker's rounding") rule that numpy, and therefore pandas Series.round,
applies. Missing values are preserved and the result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: Shift
methods.doc:
Shift returns a copy with values moved by n positions, introducing missing
values at the vacated end. A positive n shifts values toward higher
positions (down); a negative n shifts toward lower positions (up). The index
labels are unchanged, matching pandas Series.shift.

methods.name: Sort
methods.doc:
Sort returns a copy sorted by value. Missing values are placed last. When
ascending is false the order is reversed (missing values still last).

methods.name: Std
methods.doc:
Std returns the sample standard deviation (n-1 denominator) of the present
numeric values. It requires at least two values.

methods.name: Str
methods.doc:
Str returns a string accessor for the series. Values are interpreted using
their display form, so a String-dtype series works directly while other
dtypes are formatted first.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String renders the series as a simple aligned table.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc:
Sub returns the element-wise difference (s - other), aligned by position up
to the shorter length. The result dtype is Float64.

methods.name: Sum
methods.doc: Sum returns the sum of the present numeric values and whether any existed.

methods.name: Tail
methods.doc: Tail returns the last n rows.

methods.name: Take
methods.doc: Take extracts the given rows from a Series (used by GroupBy aggregation).

methods.name: Unique
methods.doc: Unique returns the distinct present values in first-seen order.

methods.name: ValueCounts
methods.doc:
ValueCounts returns a Series counting occurrences of each present value,
sorted by descending count and then by ascending value for determinism. The
resulting index holds the distinct values.

methods.name: Values
methods.doc: Values returns the values as a slice; missing entries are nil.

methods.name: Var
methods.doc:
Var returns the sample variance (n-1 denominator) of the present numeric
values and whether at least two existed.

types.name: StrAccessor
types.doc:
StrAccessor provides vectorised string operations over a Series, mirroring
the pandas Series.str accessor. Operations that return a Series preserve the
index and leave missing values missing; operations that return a boolean mask
yield false for missing or non-string values.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc:
Contains returns a boolean mask reporting, per position, whether the present
value contains the substring sub.

methods.name: EndsWith
methods.doc:
EndsWith returns a boolean mask reporting, per position, whether the present
value ends with suffix.

methods.name: Len
methods.doc:
Len returns an Int64 series giving the number of Unicode code points in each
present value; missing values remain missing.

methods.name: Lower
methods.doc: Lower returns a new series with every present value lower-cased.

methods.name: Replace
methods.doc:
Replace returns a new series with every non-overlapping occurrence of old
replaced by new in each present value.

methods.name: StartsWith
methods.doc:
StartsWith returns a boolean mask reporting, per position, whether the present
value begins with prefix.

methods.name: Strip
methods.doc:
Strip returns a new series with leading and trailing white space removed from
every present value.

methods.name: Title
methods.doc:
Title returns a new series with the first letter of each whitespace-separated
word capitalised and the remaining letters lower-cased.

methods.name: Upper
methods.doc: Upper returns a new series with every present value upper-cased.

examples.name: ReadCSV
examples.code:
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	"github.com/malcolmston/pandas"
)

func main() {
	data := "name,age,active\nAlice,30,true\nBob,25,false\n"
	df, _ := pandas.ReadCSV(strings.NewReader(data), nil)
	fmt.Printf("rows=%d cols=%d\n", df.NumRows(), df.NumCols())

	age := df.MustCol("age")
	mean, _ := age.Mean()
	fmt.Printf("mean age=%.1f dtype=%s\n", mean, age.DType())

	var b strings.Builder
	_ = df.WriteCSV(&b, nil)
	fmt.Print(b.String())

}
examples.output:
rows=2 cols=3
mean age=27.5 dtype=int64
name,age,active
Alice,30,true
Bob,25,false

examples.name: DataFrame (Describe)
examples.output:
stat,x
count,4
mean,2.5
std,1.2909944487358056
min,1
max,4

examples.name: DataFrame (Merge)
